bullets: Systematic PRISMA-compliant review of algorithmic trading, high-frequency trading, and machine learning across equity, Treasury, and foreign exchange markets, screening 847 records and synthesizing 60 studies.
No model is run; the review surveys how algorithmic agents, machine learning classifiers, and large language models affect liquidity, price discovery, and market stability, with no named model.
Characterizes markets as an algorithmic ecology and argues microstructure theory must move from rational human agents toward frameworks for correlated algorithmic failures and emergent dynamics.
High-growth ventures and entrepreneurial ecosystems across Africa, with founder-level narratives generated from unstructured online data and supplemented by primary founder interviews.
Retrieval-augmented generation with an unnamed large language model extracts founder insights into structured narratives, checked against founder interviews but without a reported accuracy figure.
AI narratives contain factual and framing errors and miss undisclosed private considerations, so AI-assisted qualitative research in data-sparse settings still requires primary triangulation.
validation_note: compared narratives to founder interviews, no accuracy figure

bullets: Conceptual multisector framework covering healthcare, insurance, law, energy, and finance, with no dataset, examining governance of generative and agentic AI systems.

No model is run; the paper adapts model risk management principles of meaningful challenge, conceptual soundness, and ongoing monitoring to non-deterministic large language models and autonomous agents.
Argues traditional model risk management remains the strongest defense against novel failure modes such as hallucination, prompt injection, and execution drift across sectors.
